rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical N-centered radical addition/semipinacol rearrangement of alkenyl cyclobutanols with sulfonimides to give beta-amino cyclic ketones (ring-expanded cyclopentanones).
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of dibenzenesulfonimide (via PCET with n-Bu4NOAc base) generates an N-centered sulfonimidyl radical; a second anodic SET oxidizes the C-radical adduct to a carbocation that triggers 1,2-alkyl shift.

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical from addition to alkene (beta-amino tertiary C-radical bearing OTMS cyclobutane)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the sulfonimide N-H via PCET (with n-Bu4NOAc as base) to give an N-centered radical I; I adds to the alkene of the alkenyl cyclobutanol giving C-radical II; anodic SET oxidizes II to cation III, which undergoes 1,2-alkyl (ring) migration (semipinacol) with loss of TMS to give the beta-amino cyclopentanone; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product observed; reaction inhibited, supporting radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Coupling product 21 isolated in 82% yield, consistent with trapping of N-centered sulfonimidyl radical.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peaks at 2.22 V (alkenyl cyclobutanol 1a) and 1.68 V (sulfonimide 2a); sulfonimide is oxidized preferentially.

notes: Mechanism proposed by analogy to prior N-centered radical addition/semipinacol work (Li/Zhang with NFSI, Xiao with aminopyridinium); electrochemistry replaces stoichiometric external oxidants. CV indicates sulfonimide (1.68 V) is oxidized before the alkene (2.22 V), supporting initial N-H oxidation rather than alkene radical cation formation.
